title: 跟穩哥展開調查
body: //曉風就是那個偷放董欣麗進來的人，而且廖哥已經發現了。 //廖哥面談到一半的時候還過來，就是想來看情況，他想看小蜂如何。 //阿姨委託曹泰銘負責調查誰是內鬼，因為他最相信曹泰銘不會做這種事，曹泰銘很理性沉穩不會感情用事。 //曹泰銘反問說曉風比較懂流程她可能比較容易找到問題，幹嘛不找她，阿姨說她太忙了不適合。她為了盯進度還時常親自下去救火，昨天她甚至要幫忙面談耶。 //目標:故意導向，真相是小蜂做的但廖哥注意到了卻漠視。 <<#If($SQ_WhoLetHerIn=0.1)>> <<#GoTo(@拒絕過問哥調查任務後回來)>> <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(10)>> ->You said you need help?;<> Tyler Cao: Yes.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外) >> <<#GoTo(@開始和曹泰銘討論秘密任務)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<@拒絕過問哥調查任務後回來>> <<#Simple(10)>> ->I changed my mind. Now I will keep your secret. Tyler Cao: Good.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外) >> <<#GoTo(@曹泰銘提到董女士的事情啦窩)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<@開始和曹泰銘討論秘密任務>> Tyler Cao: First, promise me. Whatever I'm about to say, you won't tell anyone. <<@答不答應聽調查放人事件>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I promise. Tyler Cao: Good.;<< PlayUntil (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外點頭) >> ->I thought this was about business? ($AskedIthoughtThisIsAboutBusiness);<> Tyler Cao: It is about business.;<< PlayUntil (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外點頭) >> Tyler Cao: So, will you promise? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Alright, I promise I won't tell anyone. Tyler Cao: Good. ->But why can't I tell? Tyler Cao: I can't explain unless you promise me first. Tyler Cao: What do you say? <<#GoTo(@答不答應聽調查放人事件)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->Does that mean I'm getting into some evil plan? ($AskedItMeansIHaveToCoverYou);<> Tyler Cao: Yes, but not my evil plan.;<< PlayUntil (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外點頭) >> Tyler Cao: Keeping a secret won't do you any harm, even if it's found out. Tyler Cao: What do you say? Can you make a promise? <<#GoTo(@答不答應聽調查放人事件)>> ->Sorry, but being the newbie, I'd better stay put. Tyler Cao: No problem. Come find me if you change your mind.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外返回) >> <<#End>> <<#EndSimple>> <<@曹泰銘提到董女士的事情啦窩>> Tyler Cao: There was an old lady when you were covering consultation sessions, right? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Right. Sydney Tung. Tyler Cao: Good. ->How did you know? Tyler Cao: Auntie told me. <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I'm investigating how she slipped through all our pre-screening measures.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手) >> Tyler Cao: Someone must have helped her along the way, from passing reviews to forging health records. Tyler Cao: And this someone must be an insider, a colleague of ours. As for the reason, it has to be money.;////////線索增加：對方是公司內部人員//////// Thought: If they had money to bribe farm insiders, would they bother with this contributor gig?;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Hold on. Candidates can't possibly have money. Or else they wouldn't be here. Tyler Cao: They could have money. Once signed up, they get an advance. Tyler Cao: You've worked in the contract room. Surely you must have heard about it? Thought: I see... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Now, listen, Jason. Tyler Cao: Auntie is taking this very seriously. We have a problem if more people like Sydney Tung show up at consultation. Tyler Cao: No matter how hard we try, we can't catch all of them. Tyler Cao: Eventually some who aren't physically fit will slip into the Cellar and could die. Tyler Cao: It's even worse if they survive. Chances are they will bring their similarly unfit friends. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Has this ever happened before? Tyler Cao: Not that I'm aware of. Tyler Cao: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->A huge farm like this, with truckloads of contributors going in and out, no one slipped through? ->Just curious. Tyler Cao: Yeah, it sounds unbelievable, but our automated pre-screening system is quite awesome.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外點頭) >> Tyler Cao: Being the number one supplier of flops, automation and AI is Vision's thing. Tyler Cao: Plenty of people have tried to cheat the system, but none found any success. Tyler Cao: Tung's case is kind of a big deal...;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭) >> Tyler Cao: Is it a system Glitch, like Cathy said, or what? Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Anyway, what happened, happened. Our job now is to find out how.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: My plan is to narrow down the list of suspects first. Tyler Cao: But honestly, I have no idea where to start. Thought you might have advice to give. Tyler Cao: Any idea? <<#If($WellPlayedFoundInterviewClue = 0)>> Thought: I reckon Sydney Tung was acting weird. Thought: She was surprised by me. Apparently she was to be interviewed by someone else, possibly her insider friend. <<#EndIf>> <<@泰銘問主角遇到董欣麗的經驗裡有沒有啥想法>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->She did mention being helped by a "friend". ($HerFriendHelpedIThink);<>;/////////////線索增加：對方跟董欣麗是朋友////////// Tyler Cao: Meaning they are close to each other.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭) >> Tyler Cao: Not just bribery, huh...;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭思考) >> Tyler Cao: This is important. If we can find anyone who had a personal relationship with Tung... Tyler Cao: Then we'd have pretty much solved the case. Tyler Cao: Yeah, let's begin with that.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭思考返回) >> Tyler Cao: Anything else you can think of?;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭返回) >> <<#GoTo(@泰銘問主角遇到董欣麗的經驗裡有沒有啥想法)>> ->She was expecting someone else for consultation. ($WellPlayedFoundInterviewClue);///////////線索增加：對方打算和董欣麗面談/////////////////////////// <> Tyler Cao: Ha!? Tyler Cao: This is a valuable lead! Tyler Cao: In that case, we can rule out everyone who's not available during Tung's time slot.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手) >> Tyler Cao: You did great reading Tung's responses! Tyler Cao: I know you're the right guy to ask!;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手返回) >> Tyler Cao: Anything else you can think of? <<#GoTo(@泰銘問主角遇到董欣麗的經驗裡有沒有啥想法)>> ->Sorry, that's it for now. Tyler Cao: No problem. We already have a solid lead to start with. <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Now we're official partners. Let me explain my overall schedule for this investigation! Thought: He's quite excited. Tyler Cao: Listen. Tyler Cao: All contributor candidates must pass three checks to get a consultation session. The same applies to Tung. Tyler Cao: My plan is to find out how she slipped through all three checks. Tyler Cao: If we know how they did it, we can quickly narrow down the list of suspects. <<#Simple(6)>> ->What three checks? Tyler Cao: First, all candidates must submit a copy of their own profiles.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手) >> Tyler Cao: Then Vision's pre-screening system crawls the internet to collect everything there is to know about them. Tyler Cao: The system compares everything against records from the government, such as crime, marriage, registration...;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->The government gives us access to that? Tyler Cao: I see you've never worked for a corporation of this size. Tyler Cao: Once a corporation is big enough, they gain access to everything, private information included. Tyler Cao: The government relies on big corps, either their money or tech, to govern this country. They're eager to make friends. Tyler Cao: You know what? Sometimes the government even begs us for information access. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: That's the first check. Tyler Cao: The second check is physical examination. Candidates must be assessed by clinics or hospitals appointed by Vision.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭) >> Tyler Cao: The result is then fed into the system for qualification. Tyler Cao: The final check is administrative approval. Each batch of candidates must be signed off by the division lead, the manager's secretary, and the manager.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Do they just read through this whole mountain of profiles? Tyler Cao: Of course not. The secretary and manager just sign everything off, without looking. Tyler Cao: The division lead checks random profiles for inspection. Tyler Cao: Not our division lead. Wayne hardly reads any profile these days. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Yeah, I guess he doesn't have time for it. Tyler Cao: Yep. ->Is anyone inspecting profiles for him? Tyler Cao: I don't know. You could ask him later. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: The first checks are highly automated and reliable. Cheating is virtually impossible... <<#Simple(6)>> ->Unless? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Unless someone is feeding the system incorrect information. But that is unlikely.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外低頭) >> Tyler Cao: Every entry of data is double checked and triple checked. You can't just throw in a fabricated profile and call it a day. Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Or... Are we dealing with a group? Tyler Cao: Forget about it. Let's see what we can find first.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外低頭返回) >> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Let's begin now. //資歷爬蟲蒐集>系統審核>健檢>健檢結果系統審核(評估出適配程度)>主管認證(組長、秘書、經理，但一般都不會逐條做檢查不然太費時了)>進入面談 Tyler Cao: I'll start from the first check, candidate profiles. I'm more familiar with the system. Tyler Cao: You can try physical exam records. Ask Edith for database access.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手) >> Tyler Cao: She cares a lot about contributors. Think of something related to contributor wellbeing and she'd totally buy it. Tyler Cao: Don't let her know what we're doing. Tyler Cao: Can I trust you on this?;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外抬手返回) >> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sure thing. Tyler Cao: Ha, I knew you're the man. Tyler Cao: Let's get it sorted out before anything major happens. <<(@穩哥你這樣講我壓力粉大捏>> <<#Simple(6)>> ->I'm under a ton of pressure if you have to put it that way. Tyler Cao: Haha, sorry! ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->Maybe I shouldn't do this. Tyler Cao: Hey, don't be like that! Tyler Cao: I know you could do it! Tyler Cao: I trust you! Tyler Cao: Although, we'd be in big trouble if you drop the ball. <<#GoTo(@穩哥你這樣講我壓力粉大捏)>> <<#EndSimple>> <> Quentin Qiu: Yo losers, what you guys talking about?;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭) >> Quentin Qiu: Having a good time while I'm away, huh?;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站右看) >> Tyler Cao: We're having a good time because you're away. Quentin Qiu: Am I supposed to get jealous now? Quentin Qiu: Hey Jayster, you're as cool as a cucumber alright, but don't you ever steal my BFF. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Can't help. Your BFF loves me. ->You mean, my BFF. Quentin Qiu: Gah!! Quentin Qiu: This. Means. War! Tyler Cao: Keep me out of it, you guys.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭返回) >> Quentin Qiu: Haha! You want him, eh, Jayster? Quentin Qiu: Okay, Tyler is yours. You may kiss your BFF now! Quentin Qiu: I'll just lick my wounds in my nest of pending work. Sad face. ->Actually, we've just done talking. Quentin Qiu: Ah, oh, good. Quentin Qiu: Um, you don't get my sense of humor, do you, Jayster? Tyler Cao: How is he supposed to get something that doesn't exist? Quentin Qiu: None of you get me. Sad face.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外轉頭返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Come on, dude.;<< Play (曹泰銘, MBU_曹泰銘_側坐看外返回) >>;<< Play (戴士傑, MBU_戴士傑_工廠_背站右看返回) >> <<#End>>

title: 去跟葉慈恩要健檢資料
body: //$EndingOfAskYehForAccess本段結局有四種1.主動告訴慈恩2.拖太久或太可疑被慈恩懷疑3.沒被發現完美路線4.沒被發現但慈恩記得你比較關心被殺的人 <<#Simple(10)>> ->Welcome back! It must be a long day for you. ($CareYehOnce);<> Edith Yeh: Wayne and Auntie are having a much longer day. Edith Yeh: The police are still having them for questioning. Edith Yeh: And tomorrow they'll have to face the victim's family.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Does the company require them to? Edith Yeh: No, they insist. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: Okay, bad stuff aside. Edith Yeh: Can I help you with something? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I need access to candidates' physical exam results. <<#GoTo(@跟葉慈恩恩要健檢資料喔)>> ->Nothing right now. Edith Yeh: Good.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> ->I need access to candidates' physical exam results, Ms. Yeh.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭) >> <<#GoTo(@跟葉慈恩恩要健檢資料喔)>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>> <<@跟葉慈恩恩要健檢資料喔>> Edith Yeh: Eh? What for? Thought: I can't tell her... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Tyler and I are investigating something. <<@坦承跟穩哥哥在釣一些事情>> Thought: Why did I say that!? Thought: What's the point of telling her? Edith Yeh: Oh? Something? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, it's confidential. Edith Yeh: Really? Edith Yeh: ... Thought: Even I would call myself fishy... Edith Yeh: Jason, are you... Edith Yeh: Are you guys investigating Sydney Tung? Thought: How did she know...!? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->How did you know? Edith Yeh: An informed guess. I just thought that Auntie wouldn't simply let Tung's case slide. <<#GoTo(@第一次的機會讓慈恩知道調查事件)>> ->Yes. <<#GoTo(@第一次的機會讓慈恩知道調查事件)>> ->Nope. Edith Yeh: ...If you say so. Edith Yeh: Could you tell me what this investigation is about? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Sorry, no. ->Some error in documentation, no biggie. Edith Yeh: Ah, I see. Edith Yeh: ... Thought: The way she's looking at me, I don't think she's buying it... Edith Yeh: Sure, as long as it's for work. Edith Yeh: ... Edith Yeh: Access granted. You can do whatever you need to now.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <<#EndSimple>> <>;<<#End>>;//太明顯慈恩發現私訊泰銘 <<#EndSimple>> ->We're investigating who's letting unfit candidates in. Thought: In for a dime, in for a dollar. There's no point keeping secret now... <<@第一次的機會讓慈恩知道調查事件>> Edith Yeh: ...;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回) >> Edith Yeh: Good. Thought: ...Good? Edith Yeh: I knew Auntie wouldn't just let this go. Edith Yeh: Otherwise she wouldn't deserve the manager's office. Thought: ... Thought: Um... Am I supposed to hear that? Edith Yeh: I can't tolerate shady deals like this on the farm, either, Jason.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回返回) >> Edith Yeh: We do screening for a good reason. Candidates physically unfit could be injured or even killed once racked up. Edith Yeh: That would lead to retaliation, like this mess we just had today. Edith Yeh: I'll grant you access to physical exam records. Also, I'm joining your investigation. Edith Yeh: Go check the records.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <>;<<#End>>;//直接講出來了，慈恩私訊泰銘 <<#EndSimple>> ->I want to know more about the job. Edith Yeh: Like, familiarizing with our exam record forms? Edith Yeh: I can give you a stash of blank forms for reference. It's easier that way. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Can I have a look at the profiles of candidates I interviewed today? ->No, I need the actual profiles of candidates I interviewed today. Edith Yeh: What for? <<#EndSimple>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I need real life examples of what information the clinics may provide. Edith Yeh: But you're not formally hired yet. I can't just grant you access. Sorry. Edith Yeh: You can always come back when you're officially one of us. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Okay. Truth be told, I'm investigating something, together with Tyler. <<#GoTo(@坦承跟穩哥哥在釣一些事情)>> ->Come on. Those are my candidates anyway. Edith Yeh: You're awfully persistent, huh? Thought: Now she's suspicious... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Okay. Truth be told, I'm investigating something, together with Tyler. <<#GoTo(@坦承跟穩哥哥在釣一些事情)>> ->Because I'm taking this job very seriously. Edith Yeh: Is that so... Thought: She's not buying it at all. I'm screwed... Edith Yeh: Okay, I'll grant you access. Edith Yeh: You can check the records you need on your computer.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Edith Yeh: ... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: Oh.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭) >> Edith Yeh: Are you planning something with Tyler? Edith Yeh: I saw you guys huddling together over there. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nah, we were just gossiping. Edith Yeh: ...Okay. Edith Yeh: If that's all you need, now I have work to do.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <>;<<#End>>;//拖太久被懷疑了 ->Okay. Truth be told, I'm investigating something, together with Tyler. <<#GoTo(@坦承跟穩哥哥在釣一些事情)>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I just want to double check if today's candidates are all physically fit. <<#GoTo(@慈恩聽到你關心他就會開心)>> <<#EndSimple>> ->I've got to make sure these actual people will survive the Cellar. <<@慈恩聽到你關心他就會開心>> Edith Yeh: Oh, most people wouldn't care. Edith Yeh: It's great that you do care about them, Jason. Edith Yeh: ...;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回) >> Edith Yeh: OK, now you're granted access. Edith Yeh: Check their physical exam records on your computer. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Thank you. Edith Yeh: Thank you for caring about them. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: Say, why are you asking for access now?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回返回) >> Edith Yeh: I mean, shouldn't you come to me before consultation...? Edith Yeh: Isn't it a bit late? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I just thought I should take another look. Edith Yeh: ... Edith Yeh: I see... Thought: I can't tell if she's convinced or not... <<#Simple(6)>> ->What's the matter? Edith Yeh: Nothing! Edith Yeh: I just suddenly thought of something. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Edith Yeh: Now you can go back to work.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <>;<<#End>>;//暴露路線 ->Because of what happened in the contract room. Edith Yeh: What do you mean? Thought: She's suddenly very serious. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I thought what happened to that guy must not happen again. Edith Yeh: That guy? Edith Yeh: Do you mean the attacker or the victim? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The victim, our guy. <<#GoTo(@慈恩不爽你先在意被殺的同事)>> ->The attacker. Edith Yeh: I figured. You're very empathic. Edith Yeh: His whole family died in the Cellar... Edith Yeh: We should have done a better job.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> Edith Yeh: OK, you can go back to work now. <>;<<#End>>;//沒暴露 <<#EndSimple>> ->We lost a colleague because some of us didn't do a proper job. <<@慈恩不爽你先在意被殺的同事>> Edith Yeh: You care about the victim more? Thought: Is there anything wrong with that? Why does she seem unhappy? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Of course, he died. ->No. The assaulter is just as much a victim, in a sense. Edith Yeh: I think the assaulter deserves more sympathy.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回) >> Edith Yeh: He lost his whole family. <<#Simple(99999)>> ->That he did, but our colleague lost his entire world, no? Edith Yeh: I'm not saying that he does not need sympathy. Edith Yeh: I'm just surprised by your priority.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回返回) >> Edith Yeh: I mean, one of them lost everything, while the other is well-paid by a corporate tyranny.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭低頭) >> Thought: ... Edith Yeh: Sorry, maybe I didn't sound very appropriate, but...;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭低頭返回) >> Edith Yeh: ... Edith Yeh: *Sigh* That's it. You can go back to work now, Jason.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <>;<<#End>>;//沒暴露，但葉慈恩對你印象有點不好 ->You're right. Edith Yeh: You see?;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭轉回返回) >> Edith Yeh: I knew you're an empathic man! Edith Yeh: His whole family died in the Cellar... Edith Yeh: We should have done a better job. Edith Yeh: OK, you can go back to work now.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <>;<<#End>>;//沒暴露 <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> ->We're closing shop early. I thought I could do a bit more to catch up. Edith Yeh: Oh, right. Edith Yeh: Do your work then.;<< Play (葉慈恩, MBU_葉慈恩_側坐轉頭返回) >> <>;<<#End>>;//完美路線 <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>>

title: 看健檢資料用電腦
body:
Computer: Welcome. Current user: Jason Dai.
<<@退回主畫面了看健檢資料版>>
Computer: What would you like to do?
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->[Tasks]
    Computer: You have not been assigned any tasks yet.
    Computer: Back to Home.
    <<#GoTo(@退回主畫面了看健檢資料版)>>
->[Database]
    <<@看看要看啥鬼資料看健檢資料版>>

Say the word "juicer" out loud and you will know. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Doesn't that mean she could join our secret operation? title: 小峰找廖哥吵架_自動
body: Jeff Wu: You applied for a promotion? For me? Without asking me!? George Liao: Not a promotion. It's a training course necessary for a promotion.;<> George Liao: You might not get it after all. You never know unless you try... Jeff Wu: Have you ever thought that maybe I don't want a promotion?;<> George Liao: I know you're lazy, but come on, nobody hates a promotion. Jeff Wu: What the hell!? Jeff Wu: Who the hell do you think you are, to make career decisions for me!? George Liao: I'm not making decisions for you. This is just an additional opportunity— Jeff Wu: That is called a DECISION!;<> George Liao: Take it or not, you decide. I'm just giving you an additional choice. Jeff Wu: You—;<> Jeff Wu: I said more than once! I hate this job! Jeff Wu: I think I'm helping the bad guys! This farm is hurting and killing people! George Liao: I said more than once. Grow the hell up!;<> Thought: Mr. Liao's fuming, too... George Liao: Do you know how hard it is to have a job that pays bills!?;<> George Liao: What's wrong with your job? Did they tell you to stab people or what? George Liao: Can't you just do one damn job right? Jeff Wu: You don't see the problem, because you don't care!;<> Jeff Wu: You don't care about anything other than yourself! George Liao: ... George Liao: Other than myself...? Jeff Wu: Right. Selfish and self-righteous...;<> Jeff Wu: You don't even want to help Auntie. I— George Liao: Jeff Wu. George Liao: Don't try to change the subject. Jeff Wu: ... George Liao: ...;<> George Liao: I've told you over and over again. George Liao: I care about you more than anything or anyone else in this world.;<> Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: And I told you over and over again, if you really mean it... Jeff Wu: Why don't you ever support me for once? I've had enough rejection from you. Jeff Wu: Admit it already.;<> Jeff Wu: You care about shaping me into the son that you never had. Jeff Wu: As to what I think? You don't care at all. George Liao: ...;<> George Liao: OK. Confession time, huh? George Liao: Well, here is my confession.;<> George Liao: I said I care about you. That doesn't mean I trust you. George Liao: You are a brat who has not a damn idea what he's doing with his life.;<> George Liao: I can't encourage you to shoot yourself in the foot. George Liao: You think everything is going to be OK, because you're young.;<> George Liao: All this talk about doing good for the people, for the world. George Liao: It's only because you haven't eaten enough shit to know how tough this world really is. You call me selfish? Well, I'm selfish for a reason. George Liao: But that doesn't matter.;<> George Liao: If you really hate to be selfish, I'll be selfish for you. George Liao: I'll push you toward where you need to be. Hate me for it if you want to. I don't care.;<> George Liao: You don't need to get your hands dirty, if that gives you peace of mind. Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: I don't want you to do that...;<> George Liao: I know. George Liao: I'm so tired of arguing with you.;<> George Liao: However, for this once, let's make this absolutely clear. George Liao: You're a grown man, Jeff. I can't make you do things.;<> George Liao: All I could do is talk. George Liao: You don't have to like what I said. You might even call it selfish bullshit. George Liao: Still, I must say it. Jeff Wu: Even if that will make me hate you more.;<> George Liao: Even if that will make you hate me more. Jeff Wu: I knew it...;<> Jeff Wu: You're still thinking about yourself. None of this is really about me. George Liao: If that's what you want to believe... George Liao: Suit yourself. I'm done.;<> Jeff Wu: ...Suit myself?;<> Jeff Wu: What's that supposed to mean? Jeff Wu: You're dodging the argument again. You're like that, every single time.;<> George Liao: ... Jeff Wu: Why do you keep doing this? George Liao: ...;<> Jeff Wu: Can't you face me like a man for once? George Liao: ... George Liao: What do you expect me to do, Jeff?;<> George Liao: You're already calling me an outdated, grumpy, unreasonable old geezer. George Liao: What do you expect me to say? That I am convinced by your fancy, progressive ideology? //if有解好 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=1)>> Jeff Wu: I—;<> Jeff Wu: I don't want to convince you. All I ever wanted is that you stop rejecting me, for at least once. Jeff Wu: ...;<> Jeff Wu: Is that too much to ask for? George Liao: ... George Liao: ...I'm not rejecting you.;<> George Liao: You're a good boy with good intentions.;<> George Liao: You want to help people, to have a clean life without profiting from the pain of others. Jeff Wu: I'm not—;<> George Liao: You know what? I want the same. Jeff Wu: ...?;<> George Liao: I—;<> George Liao: I'm not as selfish as you think. George Liao: I know flop farming as an industry has its problems, and I've been helping people within my power.;<> George Liao: There are things that I cannot tell you. George Liao: ...Because I'm breaking rules to do it.;<> Jeff Wu: ...What? George Liao: Believe me, for just once, Jeff. George Liao: I'm not what you'd call a selfish man. Jeff Wu: I—;<> Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: I know you're not. I always knew. Jeff Wu: It's just that— Jeff Wu: Sorry... Jeff Wu: I don't want to disappoint you. At the same time, I can't give up my values, either. body: //第二日頂樓對談結束回篩選組，在辦公室門外遇到靠強在等主角的穩哥 <> Tyler Cao: Hey, Jason.;<> Tyler Cao: You're back. Tyler Cao: Can I borrow you for a minute? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What's up? // ->You've been waiting for me? Tyler Cao: However, the introduction of these hospitals begs a new question.;<> Tyler Cao: Normally people buy these forged reports for insurance claims. No one ever tried to bring them to a flop farm. Tyler Cao: Because—;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Because Vision's pre-screening is airtight. Tyler Cao: Well, yes, but it's not the primary reason. Will let you know when it's ready.;<> Thought: Tyler seems to be very troubled...? <<#Simple(8)>> ->Meanwhile, can I do something for you? Tyler Cao: Nothing yet. But as ever— ->What's up, Tyler? Tyler Cao: Huh? What?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Never mind. Tyler Cao: Yeah.;<> ->Something on your mind? Tyler Cao: No, just a bit tired, is all.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Oh. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Please don't tell anyone about this.;<> Tyler Cao: We don't want to alert the culprit. Tyler Cao: I will make a list of everyone who could have taken Tung's session. Tyler Cao: Based on that, we will figure out what to do. body: Jeff Wu: Huh? Jeff Wu: Hey, Tyler and Jason. Can I help you guys? Tyler Cao: Jeff... Jeff Wu: What?;<> Jeff Wu: What's with the long face? Haha... Tyler Cao: Did you let Sydney Tung slip through screening? Jeff Wu: ...;<> Tyler Cao: You planned to give her the green light during consultation. Then she'd go to the Cellar. Jeff Wu: I have no idea what you're talking about, man. Thought: Your face suggests otherwise, dude... Jeff Wu: Hey, wait...;<> Jeff Wu: Why are you suddenly talking about this? Tyler Cao: Auntie asked me to investigate.;<> Jeff Wu: But Auntie is now—;<> Tyler Cao: Exactly. Now HQ is taking the case over.;<> Jeff Wu: HQ!? Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: All you have is an assumption. Any hard evidence? No. Tyler Cao: Jeff... Jeff Wu: I know I'm your prime suspect!;<> Jeff Wu: I'm the only one with a chance to take Tung's session, other than Jason. Jeff Wu: But— Tyler Cao: Listen to me, Jeff. Mr. Liao just came to me and said he's helping Tung. Jeff Wu: Mr. Liao? How could he...!?;<> Tyler Cao: But the truth is you did it, am I right? Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: It's me.;<> Tyler Cao: ...;<>;<> Tyler Cao: *Sigh* Jeff Wu: What should I do now, Tyler? Help me!;<>;<> Jeff Wu: I can't let him take the fall for me!;<> Tyler Cao: Calm down, Jeff. Tyler Cao: His confession means nothing, if you step up now. Jeff Wu: Good... Tyler Cao: But to invalidate his confession, I will need your side of the story, the whole truth. Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: Ms. Tung helped us a lot.;<> Jeff Wu: A few days ago she came to George, saying she needed money to visit her grandson. Jeff Wu: As we all know, George doesn't have that much money to spare. So she asked for a contributor job... Jeff Wu: But George said no. Jeff Wu: She was heartbroken. So I thought... Jeff Wu: ... Tyler Cao: Why was Mr. Liao hanging around when Jason was interviewing Tung? Just coincidence? I'm not buying it.;<> Jeff Wu: ...He wanted to stop me.;<> Jeff Wu: Assigning her to Jason was his doing, too. Jeff Wu: The idea was that if she's not admitted, no one would find out my little trick. Jeff Wu: Looks like it's not that simple, though... Tyler Cao: ... Jeff Wu: What's the punishment for me? Tyler Cao: ...I don't know yet. Tyler Cao: But chances are you won't be able to keep this job. Jeff Wu: Huh...;<> Jeff Wu: But George will be left alone, yes? Tyler Cao: Yes. Jeff Wu: Good.;<> Jeff Wu: I will talk to him, then. Jeff Wu: It's not right to ruin his life because of my shenanigans. Jeff Wu: You just write the report based on the truth, Tyler. Tyler Cao: ...OK. Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: George Liao, old grumpy guy with a poopy face.;<> Jeff Wu: You know what? He was not like that before. Once he was a very helpful not-so-old guy. Jeff Wu: Annoyingly so. He used to actively offer help to anyone who might need it, like some sort of salesman of good intentions. Jeff Wu: ...;<> Jeff Wu: Then I don't know what happened. Suddenly he became this selfish man. Tyler Cao: Jeff... Jeff Wu: No, I happened. He became what he is because of me. Tyler Cao: ... Jeff Wu: My mom passed away, leaving me to George. He raised me like his own son.;<> Jeff Wu: He actually could afford to lend Ms. Tung the money she needed. Jeff Wu: But all his savings were set a side in an emergency fund for me. Jeff Wu: I did argue with him, tell him that I don't need the money. Unsurprisingly, he wouldn't listen. //if廖哥小峰好結局 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>> Jeff Wu: We've been having fights, yeah. Jeff Wu: You just saw one yourself, Jason. Haha...;<> Jeff Wu: Anyway, I've said my piece. Jeff Wu: I think we know each other a lot better than before, George and me. Jeff Wu: I broke the rules to let Ms. Tung through. Of course I'd take the consequences.;<> <<#GoTo(@跳過完全沒解廖哥小峰的可能性)>> <<#EndIf>> //if廖哥小峰壞結局 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=-2)>> Jeff Wu: We've been having fights, yeah. Jeff Wu: You just saw one yourself, Jason. Haha...;<> Jeff Wu: Anyway, I've said my piece. Jeff Wu: Between George and I, there should be a healthy distance, like, what's his and what's mine. Jeff Wu: In this case, the consequences are all mine. He has no right to take them for me. Jeff Wu: He wants to protect me, alright, but that's for the peace of his own conscience.;<> Jeff Wu: I won't allow him to manipulate my life anymore, for good or bad. Jeff Wu: I take my own consequences. This is a good start. <<#GoTo(@跳過完全沒解廖哥小峰的可能性)>> <<#EndIf>> //if沒解廖哥小峰支線 Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: Sorry for boring you out.;<> Jeff Wu: Now that you've had my confession, I guess it's case closed? Tyler Cao: Yes. Jeff Wu: I made the decision, I take the consequence, fair and square.;<> // <<@跳過完全沒解廖哥小峰的可能性>> Jeff Wu: Let's see what the HQ wants to do with me. Tyler Cao: I...;<> Jeff Wu: Don't you blame yourself, Tyler Cao.;<> Jeff Wu: I screwed myself up royally. Simple as that. Tyler Cao: ...;<> Jeff Wu: Ha! I knew you're blaming yourself.;<> Jeff Wu: Chillax, dude.;<> Jeff Wu: Now if you folks excuse me, I have work to do.;<> Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: I see. Tyler Cao: Thank you, Jeff. <> //回小峰位子 <<#Wait(5)>> Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: I'm glad that we had a talk with Jeff. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why's that? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I'd regret it for a lifetime if we didn't hear him out.;<> Tyler Cao: And what he said at the end of it... Tyler Cao: That took a massive load off me. Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Now I'm sure we have the best of all possible outcomes.;<> Tyler Cao: Thank you, Jason. Tyler Cao: I owe you one. Tyler Cao: Let me know if I can ever return this favor. <<#Simple(8)>> ->No need. Tyler Cao: Haha, maybe for now. Just remember that the offer stands. ->I'll make a note of it. Tyler Cao: Yeah, you do that. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I'll go back to work then. The report won't write itself. Tyler Cao: I guess you have work to do, as well. Tyler Cao: See you later. <> <<#End>>

title: 第二次賴潔和陳家騏
body: Kaylee Chen: Jesse.;<> Jesse Lai: ... Kaylee Chen: Jesse...? Jesse Lai: What?;<> Kaylee Chen: Now that we're at work... Kaylee Chen: There are a few things that I need your advice on. Jesse Lai: OK. Sure. Kaylee Chen: First, the surveillance system...;<> Jesse Lai: You got the whole architecture wrong. Jesse Lai: You thought all modules must be tightly integrated, right? Nope. A series of independent sub-systems works better.;<> Kaylee Chen: Hmm... Jesse Lai: Does that solve your problem? Kaylee Chen: Kind of. Jesse Lai: Is that yes or no? Jesse Lai: Don't you "kind of" me. Kaylee Chen: ... Kaylee Chen: Yes. You did solve my problem. Jesse Lai: Cool. What's next? Kaylee Chen: Next is the network topology... Jesse Lai: You've been on a wrong track altogether.;<> Jesse Lai: I saw your work. There's a lot of redundancy. Jesse Lai: It's kind of fascinating why you'd want to loop data forth and back like that. Jesse Lai: Very interesting, but a stupid thing to do nonetheless. Don't do it next time.;<> Kaylee Chen: ... Jesse Lai: ... Jesse Lai: I— Jesse Lai: I'm sorry. My attitude is out of control again...;<> Jesse Lai: *Sigh* Jesse Lai: I know I'm a dick, alright. Kaylee Chen: ...I don't blame you. Kaylee Chen: You must be overwhelmed by the issues of the Cellar.;<> //IF許大哥有解開 <<#If(KnowTruthControlDepartment=0)>> Kaylee Chen: And on top of that, Mr. Hsu... <<#EndIf>> Jesse Lai: No, it's not like that. I just—;<> Jesse Lai: I'm just like that.;<> Jesse Lai: This is the reason why I'm dead against working with you guys as a team. Jesse Lai: Patience is not my strongest suit. I lose my temper very easily. Jesse Lai: All my past colleagues hate me. They call me arrogant, control freak, so on and so forth.;<> Kaylee Chen: ... Kaylee Chen: If you know it's a problem, why don't you... Jesse Lai: Why don't I fix it?;<> Jesse Lai: Because it's hard. Jesse Lai: I know this sounds arrogant, but I've got to say it anyway...;<> Jesse Lai: The thing is, I'm smarter than most people, at least in my domain of expertise. Jesse Lai: To me, the mistakes they make are super obvious. Jesse Lai: Still, they make the same mistakes over and over again.;<> Jesse Lai: Keep going the wrong way, and whine like a crybaby when they run into a wall face first. Jesse Lai: Then they come to me for help, bitching about everything except for their own stupidity. Jesse Lai: I could have pointed out their mistakes early on, but they'd call me rude or manipulative in that case.;<> Jesse Lai: So I have to shut up and watch them make a fool of themselves. Jesse Lai: It's the same even after they screw up... Jesse Lai: So they ask me to fix stuff for them, but I can't tell them that they've been on the wrong track since the beginning. Jesse Lai: Instead, I've got to babysit them, cater for their feelings.;<> Kaylee Chen: ... Jesse Lai: Now you see I'm a dick. Kaylee Chen: No, I don't think so. Kaylee Chen: I'm just wondering that this "they" of yours, are their feelings really so fragile? Jesse Lai: ...Depends on what issue they have.;<> Jesse Lai: Like, if it's trivial stuff that they don't really mind, I can't just go and point out their mistakes. Jesse Lai: That would definitely make me a prick.;<> Jesse Lai: On the other hand, if it's important stuff, they kind of take it very personally. Jesse Lai: Criticizing their methodology is often taken as a personal attack.;<> Jesse Lai: Most of them don't have the objective perspective to see the difference. Jesse Lai: If I have a problem with the way they do things, I'm challenging their authority, that sort of thing.;<> Kaylee Chen: What if you take a not so straightforward approach? Jesse Lai: Nah, don't have the patience for that.;<> Jesse Lai: Like, dancing around the elephant in the room. Jesse Lai: Point out the problem without pointing out that they are the problem. Jesse Lai: Help them out and make them feel good. Nope, can't do that. Kaylee Chen: ... Jesse Lai: You think I'm exaggerating.;<> Kaylee Chen: Somewhat. Jesse Lai: Well, told you I'm a dick. Jesse Lai: This is who I am. I've been trying to polish myself.;<> Jesse Lai: Although it's not quite working, as you can tell. Jesse Lai: So, I'm going for the next best thing.;<> Kaylee Chen: Which is, never work with other people. Jesse Lai: Yep. Jesse Lai: If there's no teamwork, I don't get a chance to show my prick face.;<> Jesse Lai: ... Jesse Lai: Sorry for being a nuisance.;<> Jesse Lai: The advice I gave, I'll rephrase them better. Jesse Lai: As to now... Jesse Lai: I need some alone time. Jesse Lai: I suppose you're right. The Cellar is stressing me out alright. Kaylee Chen: ...OK. Kaylee Chen: ...We can talk about it later.;<> Jesse Lai: ...;<> Kaylee Chen: ... Jesse Lai: Kaylee. Kaylee Chen: Yes?;<> Jesse Lai: Thank you. Kaylee Chen: For what? Jesse Lai: For not hating me on the spot. Kaylee Chen: ... Kaylee Chen: You know what? I kind of like you. Jesse Lai: Huh...?;<> Kaylee Chen: Anyway, let's get the work done first.;<> Jesse Lai: Oh, right... <> <> <<#End>>

Jesse Lai: Scientists have always been trying to create a human-like artificial intelligence, right?;<> Jesse Lai: They're doing everything they can to make AI behave closer to humans, as if it's a form of evolution. Jesse Lai: Truth is, human and artificial intelligences are fundamentally different. Jesse Lai: The root of all differences is that we humans can't focus on one single objective. Jesse Lai: An AI, on the other hand, is created for one purpose, and one purpose only.;<> Jesse Lai: Usually it's to serve us humans in one way or another. Jesse Lai: Sometimes the AI fails to comprehend what we need. We call it "artificial idiot" because of that. Jesse Lai: However, in most of these cases, the root cause is that we can't articulate exactly what we need.;<> Jesse Lai: The AI doesn't have a clear objective to start with. What can it do? Make random guesses, of course. Jesse Lai: In that sense... Right? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->R-ight...? ->No...? <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: Okay. Here is where our opinions differ.;<> Jesse Lai: Say, what is the human mind to an AI? Jesse Lai: A higher form of being, or an outdated heap of junk? Kaylee Chen: I believe AI doesn't need to mimic human thinking. Jesse Lai: Just think, Jason.;<> Jesse Lai: From our perspective, a lot of decisions made by AI are incomprehensible. Jesse Lai: They do things that seem ridiculous, but that's only an attempt to fulfill the sole purpose they're assigned.;<> Jesse Lai: This purpose is assigned by humans. Right? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->R-ight? Jesse Lai: Yes!;<> ->No? Jesse Lai: What do you mean by no? Jesse Lai: Of course yes!;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Oh. ->Why did you ask, if it's not a question? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: Never mind! <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: How do humans perform in terms of fulfilling purposes? Jesse Lai: Ironically, humans are also the only species that could commit suicide.;<> Jesse Lai: In that sense, the human mind is no different from a crappy AI. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wait. I wouldn't say humans live for the purpose of living. Jesse Lai: Interesting.;<> Jesse Lai: What is the purpose of life, in your opinion? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Enjoy life. ->Seek knowledge. ->I don't know, but it can't be just living. <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: Maybe. Jesse Lai: But that is a very subjective idea, no?;<> Jesse Lai: It's an opinion, not a fact. We invented these purposes ourselves. Jesse Lai: This is my point.;<> Jesse Lai: The so-called meaning of life, the purpose of human civilization... Jesse Lai: If we get to the bottom of it, it's just a ball of chaos.;<> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: What is the human mind? Jesse Lai: Layers upon layers of noises, is what it is.;<> Jesse Lai: Think about it.;<> Jesse Lai: The life of an average human is utterly distracted. Focusing on one single thing is impossible.;<> Jesse Lai: Because we care about too many things. Jesse Lai: Plus!;<>;<> Jesse Lai: There are random factors affecting our thinking, such as the weather, the mood, so on and so forth. Jesse Lai: All these factors combined make each of us unique.;<> Jesse Lai: But ultimately, this uniqueness has no meaning.;<> Jesse Lai: We're just constructs made unnecessarily complicated. Jesse Lai: Still, we worship this complicatedness, inventing names for it. We call it the soul, the free will. Jesse Lai: Does it have any value?;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Probably not? Jesse Lai: Right!;<> ->Does anything have any value? Jesse Lai: That's a separate question. Jesse Lai: Let's focus on our discussion here. Jesse Lai: I define "value" as the ability to exert influence on the physical world. Kaylee Chen: Hey, you're wandering away. Jesse Lai: Ah, right.;<> ->I beg to differ. Kaylee Chen: Good judgement. Jesse Lai: Hey!;<> <<#EndSimple>> Kaylee Chen: Now it's my turn. Kaylee Chen: Let's reuse Jesse's example of slippery slope.;<>;<> Jesse Lai: It's not a slippery slope! Kaylee Chen: She said the purpose of life is to keep on living. Kaylee Chen: I say this will to live is a result, not a reason. Kaylee Chen: Who is in a position to give a definition to the meaning of life?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Gods, maybe. Kaylee Chen: Gods don't exist. Jesse Lai: I have friends in multiple religions ready to argue with you over that. Kaylee Chen: They don't have to agree with me.;<> Jesse Lai: I like your attitude. Ha!;<> Kaylee Chen: As far as we know, only humans believe the purpose of life is to reproduce.;<> Kaylee Chen: Only humans believe life is better than death.;<> Jesse Lai: Some aliens might agree with humans. Kaylee Chen: Don't introduce your subjective aliens yet! Jesse Lai: Okay, haha. ->Humans? Kaylee Chen: Correct. <<#EndSimple>> Kaylee Chen: We may not be in a position to give a verdict, but we do believe it nonetheless. Kaylee Chen: Humans, though, are not one single being. Kaylee Chen: Opinions differ, especially over things that are pure speculation. Kaylee Chen: For example, I could propose that the ultimate meaning of life is to...;<> Kaylee Chen: ...to create a technology that loops time itself back to the moment of the Big Bang. A perfect reset. <> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Wut? Jesse Lai: Wut? ->Yeah, sure, interesting. Jesse Lai: Haha, you're losing him.;<> Kaylee Chen: Let me finish. <> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Kaylee Chen: Anyway, the notion that living beings exist to reproduce is a stretched theory.;<> Kaylee Chen: In a sense, everything in the known universe are the same... Kaylee Chen: They exist because they already exist. There's no prescriptive meaning in it.;<> Jesse Lai: Ah, nihilism.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->I feel you're kind of off the subject? Kaylee Chen: No. It's all related. ->Meaning there's no difference whether we live or die? Kaylee Chen: No. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Kaylee Chen: If there's no assigned meaning, we should invent our own meaning.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Hold on. Are we back to square one now? Kaylee Chen: We are, but there's a subtle difference... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Kaylee Chen: Every each of us should find a meaning to their own life.;<> Kaylee Chen: AI doesn't have such mental faculty. Kaylee Chen: It's focused and efficient, yet not a living thing.;<> Kaylee Chen: Does the invention of automobiles make the existence of horses meaningless? Jesse Lai: Hmph. Kaylee Chen: What? Jesse Lai: In your logic, as long as an AI is capable of finding a purpose for its own existence...;<> Jesse Lai: Instead of waiting to be assigned a purpose... Jesse Lai: It would transcend humans? Kaylee Chen: That's the thing. AI can't do that. Jesse Lai: Just wait and see. I'm pretty sure someone will create an AI capable of exactly that in a few years.;<> Jesse Lai: By then you'll have to agree with me. Kaylee Chen: Do you have to spread this existential crisis? Jesse Lai: Existential crisis is already spreading, with or without me. I might as well give it a push. Kaylee Chen: You'll got to hell for that. Jesse Lai: Well, then I go to hell. Jesse Lai: What do you think, Jason?;<> Jesse Lai: Who's making more sense? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Jesse. Jesse Lai: As expected.;<> Kaylee Chen: ... Kaylee Chen: Care to explain? <> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->The human mind is indeed compounded layers of noises. ->If free will can be defined, it's not truly free. Kaylee Chen: ... Kaylee Chen: I see your point. ->Nothing to explain. It's just a feeling. Kaylee Chen: ...Huh? Jesse Lai: Ha! A feeling will do! <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: I have his vote! That's what matters!;<> Kaylee Chen: ...Then we need more votes.;<> Jesse Lai: Huh? Didn't you say we should leave the others out of it? Kaylee Chen: I changed my mind. Jesse Lai: Hahaha!;<> ->Kaylee. Jesse Lai: Huh? Kaylee Chen: Sensible. Jesse Lai: Why? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Survival is not a purpose of us. Kaylee Chen: Each of us must choose our own purpose, right! ->AI can't possibly have self-awareness. <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: Hmph.;<> Kaylee Chen: Time to admit defeat.;<> Jesse Lai: ... Jesse Lai: No. Let's try someone else!;<> Kaylee Chen: Again?;<> Jesse Lai: Yep!;<> ->I'm not picking sides. Jesse Lai: Hey! Don't cheat! Kaylee Chen: You shouldn't have forced him to listen to our nonsense to begin with.;<> Jesse Lai: Your nonsense. I'm making perfect sense.;<> Kaylee Chen: Sorry Jason. I should have put a leash on Jesse.;<> Jesse Lai: I'm not your dog! <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: Anyway, thank you for wasting your time, Jason.;<> Kaylee Chen: Thank you for keeping Jesse entertained. <> //if賴潔陳家騏事件有解完 <<#If($SQ_LaijieJiaChi=3)>> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->It's nothing. // ->My brain isn't built for this... Jesse Lai: A sign of healthy, active thinking. Kaylee Chen: Agreed. ->How is it going between you and Jesse? ($DLStateFloat) Kaylee Chen: She finally agreed to go out with me. Jesse Lai: No, I didn't...!?;<> Kaylee Chen: Does that mean you're not going?;<> Jesse Lai: No-nope. Kaylee Chen: Yeah, that's how it's going.;<> Kaylee Chen: Since you brought that up... Kaylee Chen: I want to say thank you, Jason. Kaylee Chen: Jesse wouldn't be fully open with me if not for you.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->It's her own choice. Kaylee Chen: You're right. Kaylee Chen: Heh heh. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: Enough talk about me! <<#EndSimple>> <<@跟賴潔陳家騏進行工廠最後道別欸>> Jesse Lai: See you later! <<#Simple(8)>> ->See you later. // ->It's been an honor working with you. Kaylee Chen: It's been an honor for me, too. Jesse Lai: ... Kaylee Chen: What's up, Jesse? Jesse Lai: Why do you make it sound like you will be gone for good? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->No, I don't mean that. Jesse Lai: ...Oh. Kaylee Chen: Don't overreact. ->Yeah, I may not come back. Kaylee Chen: ...What? Jesse Lai: Don't ask.;<> Kaylee Chen: But you— Jesse Lai: He would tell us if he could. Jesse Lai: Anyway, wish you the best, whatever you're dealing with.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Thank you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jesse Lai: OK, then. Jesse Lai: We will meet again, I hope. ->... <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#End>>

They are mean. Jesse Lai: I know there are thousands of nicer ways to delivery my opinions, but I—;<> Jesse Lai: I just go for the bluntest, nastiest one, every single time. Jesse Lai: Because I don't have the patience. Jesse Lai: I can't tolerate incompetent people. Jesse Lai: ...;<> Jesse Lai: There, I did it again, calling people incompetent. Jesse Lai: It's becoming an endless loop. Jesse Lai: ...;<> Kaylee Chen: ... Kaylee Chen: You're too smart, Jesse. A part of being too smart is that you think too much.;<> Kaylee Chen: You can tell something is going wrong way before it actually does. Kaylee Chen: It's kind of a curse. Kaylee Chen: In your eyes, the world is full of stupid, totally avoidable mistakes. But you can't do anything about them. Kaylee Chen: Eventually you'd become either a nihilist or a cynic. Jesse Lai: ...Obviously I'm the latter. ->...

body:
//2.謝采欣:坐在自己的位子
//    篩選失敗:坐在自己的位子
//    篩選勝利:坐在自己的位子(吃東西)
//    雙贏:坐在自己的位子(吃東西)



Cathy Hsieh: Ah, Jason.

//if篩選失敗
<<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: ...Are you alright?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->What?
        Cathy Hsieh: You and Wayne went to see the CEO, no?
        Cathy Hsieh: I guess things didn't go too well?
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->Oh, it's not that bad.
        ->Kind of.
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->It went quite OK.
        Cathy Hsieh: Good to know.
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Poor Wayne. He almost made it.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Yeah.
        //
    ->There's always the next time.
        Cathy Hsieh: Yes!
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Hey, I've never welcomed you to Screening.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看返回)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Haha, the timing is awkward and all...
    Cathy Hsieh: But I'm glad to have you on our side!
    Cathy Hsieh: Welcome to Screening!
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Thank you, Cathy.
        Cathy Hsieh: Yes!
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
//if篩選成功或雙贏
<<#If($FabWinWin=1||$FabWinWin=0)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Did you try the pizza QQ ordered? It's yummy!
    Cathy Hsieh: Coriander pork blood pizza! And stinky tofu topping!;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐披薩)>>
    Thought: Uh...
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Sounds great.
        Cathy Hsieh: Right!?;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐披薩返回)>>
    ->That...weird.
        Cathy Hsieh: Don't be intimidated by the name! Take a bit and you'll love it!;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐披薩返回)>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: ...
    Cathy Hsieh: Say...
    //if雙贏
    <<#If($FabWinWin=0)>>
        Cathy Hsieh: I'm surprised that Justin agreed to work with Wayne.
        Cathy Hsieh: Going forward, Silencio will only get better, right?
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->I think so.
            Cathy Hsieh: Yes!
        ->I'm not that optimistic.
            Cathy Hsieh: ...Right. We have plenty of unsolved problems.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看)>>
            Cathy Hsieh: But I'm confident!
            Cathy Hsieh: As long as they are on the same side, there's nothing they can't tackle.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐右看返回)>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
    <<#EndIf>>
    //if篩選單贏
    <<#If($FabWinWin=1)>>
        Cathy Hsieh: From here on, Wayne will be the farm manager?
        <<#Simple(99999)>>
        ->Yeah.
            //
        ->If Shane Yan doesn't break his promise.
            Cathy Hsieh: Will he? He's the CEO.
            <<#Simple(99999)>>
            ->I don't think he will.
                Cathy Hsieh: Yeah.
            ->Hard to say.
                Cathy Hsieh: You don't seem to trust him much...
            <<#EndSimple>>
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Cathy Hsieh: ...
        Cathy Hsieh: I wonder how Justin is dealing with it...
        Cathy Hsieh: A lot of Ops people will resign, I guess.
        <<#Simple(8)>>
        ->Why?
            Cathy Hsieh: I know quite a few of them. They won't stay.
        ->That can't be helped.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
        Cathy Hsieh: ...
        Cathy Hsieh: Let's do what we can and hope for the best.
    <<#EndIf>>
<<#EndIf>>

//if有解采欣任務成功
<<#If($BallTsaiShinEventStatus=4)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Hey, I almost forgot!
    Cathy Hsieh: I had a talk with QQ.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐手指)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->How did it go?
        //
    ->Looks like it went well?
        Cathy Hsieh: Yeah.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: He doesn't quite understand my concerns.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐手指返回)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: But I could see that he's trying to avoid stressing me more.
    Cathy Hsieh: He said he never cared about equal abilities and whatnot.
    Cathy Hsieh: And that he's sorry for making me feel bad.
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->How do you feel now?
        Cathy Hsieh: I'm grateful, I guess.
        Cathy Hsieh: Grateful that QQ doesn't mind.
    ->So, you're not going to Ops anymore?
        Cathy Hsieh: I will keep going.
        Cathy Hsieh: I'm still forcing myself to grow, but for a different reason.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Anyway, it feels great to finally talk about it with someone.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐抬手)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: It's also great to find out that QQ has a big heart!
    Cathy Hsieh: ...;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐抬手返回)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Wait, am I saying too many nice things about him?
    Cathy Hsieh: Don't tell him! Or else he will brag about it for years!
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Sure. Haha.
        //
    ->Nope, I'm telling him every single word.
        Cathy Hsieh: Hey!
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Hahaha!
<<#EndIf>>
//if解采欣任務失敗
<<#If($BallTsaiShinEventStatus=2||$BallTsaiShinEventStatus=3)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Oh.
    Cathy Hsieh: I've made peace with QQ.;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐摸頭)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Thank you for being the messenger.
    Cathy Hsieh: ...;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐摸頭返回)>>
    //if有聽完采欣焦慮但沒有鼓勵去找球球
    <<#If($BallTsaiShinEventStatus=3)>>
        Cathy Hsieh: I still care a lot about who's better and such...
        Cathy Hsieh: But I'll focus on catching up with him, instead of brooding over it.
        Cathy Hsieh: Yeah, I will, maybe. Haha...
    <<#EndIf>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Anyway, that's the situation now, in case you want to know.
<<#EndIf>>
//if雙贏
<<#If($FabWinWin=0)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Hey, have you seen Wayne and Justin?
        Cathy Hsieh: Eh? No, I haven't.
        Cathy Hsieh: Sorry.
        <<#Simple(6)>>
        ->No problem.
        ->...
        <<#EndSimple>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
Cathy Hsieh: Okay.
//if雙贏或單贏
<<#If($FabWinWin=0||$FabWinWin=1)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: Now it's time to enjoy my pizza!;<<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐披薩)>>
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Aren't you joining their conversations?
        Cathy Hsieh: I talk to them every day, but this pizza won't last!
    ->Don't let me keep you, then.
        Cathy Hsieh: I don't mind, haha.
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
    <<Play(謝采欣,MBU_謝采欣_背坐披薩返回)>>
<<#EndIf>>
//if單輸
<<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>>
    Cathy Hsieh: I have work to do.
<<#EndIf>>

Cathy Hsieh: Now, if you excuse me.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->See you later.
    Cathy Hsieh: Sure.
->It's been an honor working with you. Goodbye.
    Cathy Hsieh: Huh? Eh?
    Cathy Hsieh: Why so formal?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Nothing.
        Cathy Hsieh: Oh.
        Cathy Hsieh: So...
    ->Just feel like saying it.
        Cathy Hsieh: You're weird, Jason.
        Cathy Hsieh: Hahaha.
    <<#EndSimple>>
    Cathy Hsieh: It's been an honor working with you, too.
    Cathy Hsieh: See you later, then.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<#End>>

title: 結局對話:曹泰銘跟吳晉峰
body: //3.曹泰銘 // 篩選失敗:在小峰位子 // 篩選勝利:在小峰位子 // 雙贏:在小峰位子 //7.吳晉峰 // 篩選失敗:坐在自己的位子 // 篩選勝利:坐在自己的位子 // 雙贏:坐在自己的位子 Jeff Wu: Hey, Jason. Tyler Cao: Hi. //if篩選失敗 <<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>> Jeff Wu: ...Aren't you concerned? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->About what? Jeff Wu: The future of Silencio. ->Not at all. Jeff Wu: You have a big heart, then. ->More or less. Jeff Wu: Right... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Today we managed to buy some more time...;<> Tyler Cao: Eventually we will have to deal with this farm closure issue again. Jeff Wu: Hmm... Tyler Cao: How would you feel if this farm is shut down, Jeff?;<> Jeff Wu: Huh? Why ask me? Tyler Cao: You always doubted the social impact of flop farms, right? Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: I don't know.;<> Jeff Wu: The fact that I'm still here means I believe this farm does more good than harm. Jeff Wu: If it's shut down, I guess I will feel sorry. Jeff Wu: Plus, I would miss you guys.;<> Tyler Cao: ... Jeff Wu: Haha... Jeff Wu: Now, don't be so gloomy. Let's celebrate!;<> <<#EndIf>> //if篩選成功或雙贏 <<#If($FabWinWin=1||$FabWinWin=0)>> Jeff Wu: QQ is out of his mind. Did you see what pizza he ordered?;<> Jeff Wu: Coriander pork blood pizza? Stinky tofu topping? Tyler Cao: Uh, it's not so bad? Jeff Wu: Not so bad? If words get out, Italy will declare war on us!;<> Tyler Cao: Hahaha... Jeff Wu: Wayne is the manager now. He should make some pizza rules. Tyler Cao: That would be an abuse of power. //if篩選成功 <<#If($FabWinWin=1)>> Jeff Wu: At least we have the power! Finally, we have defeated Ops! Tyler Cao: Yeah. Now we can start to make Silencio better.;<> Jeff Wu: Now we should be able to lower screening standards. Jeff Wu: Yes, without Ops standing in our way.;<> Tyler Cao: Right. A lot of people desperately need this job.;<> Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: I wonder how Ops is dealing with this news.;<> Tyler Cao: You think they will push back? Jeff Wu: Of course they will. Jeff Wu: You're the one who's worked with them. What do you think, Jason?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->A large part of the team will quit. Jeff Wu: Wow...;<> Tyler Cao: ...That's not good news.;<> Tyler Cao: Silencio needs them. Jeff Wu: Yeah.;<> <> ->They won't be happy about it, but have to cooperate anyway. Tyler Cao: That would be good.;<> Jeff Wu: Yeah. Wayne is going to be the manager, but we won't make their life any harder than necessary. Jeff Wu: Like it or not, they will have to take orders from us anyway.;<> ->I don't know. Jeff Wu: ...Okay. <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //if雙贏 <<#If($FabWinWin=0)>> Jeff Wu: Hey, seriously.;<> Jeff Wu: It's so great to have Screening and Ops working together. Jeff Wu: Silencio isn't truly functional unless all divisions are united. Jeff Wu: The original plan was that we grab leadership, suppressing Ops if they raise any objection.;<>;<> Jeff Wu: We could make it work, but there would be lots of problems. Jeff Wu: This is more than we could ever have asked for.;<> Tyler Cao: Screening is still taking the lead.;<> Jeff Wu: Yeah, but I guess Wayne will want to treat the Ops lead as his equal. <<#EndIf>> Jeff Wu: With Wayne promoted, who's gonna fill his position? Tyler Cao: Hmm... Tyler Cao: In terms of seniority, it has to be Mr. Liao.;<> Jeff Wu: Huh? George? Please, no! Tyler Cao: Haha... Tyler Cao: Edith has a shot, too. I heard she was a candidate for Screening lead, competing against Wayne. Jeff Wu: That's a lot better than George. Tyler Cao: Let's see how Wayne deals with it. <> <<#EndIf>> //if小峰路線解成功 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>> Jeff Wu: Oh, Jason. Jeff Wu: I, um... Jeff Wu: *Sigh* <<#Simple(6)>> ->You what? ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: George said... Jeff Wu: He said...;<> Tyler Cao: Can you finish your sentence or not? Jeff Wu: George wants to invite you to dinner, because you helped us so much.;<> <<#Simple(8)>> ->Ugh. Jeff Wu: I know, right? I was like "ugh" when he brought it up! ->Helped you? How? Jeff Wu: Didn't you help us address our dispute? //if篩選組 <<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>> Jeff Wu: Also, he said you and Wayne went out of your way to help him when HQ took him away. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Tyler helped, too. Tyler Cao: Hey, leave me out of it! ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: Well, this is awkward. Jeff Wu: I mean, with George at the table, what can we talk about during the dinner? Nothing! It's gonna be so weird. Jeff Wu: Wait, no. He will lecture us from beginning to finish!;<> Jeff Wu: Ahhhhh kill me already! Tyler Cao: Hahaha! Jeff Wu: Anyway, he's gonna invite you sooner or later. Don't say I didn't warn you.;<> Jeff Wu: Just tell him no if you want to. That would be doing everyone a huge favor! Jeff Wu: Although I guess it can be hard to say no to an old man, haha... <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I actually would like to go. Jeff Wu: For real?;<> Jeff Wu: Whatever. Your call. <> ->Let's see how it goes. Tyler Cao: That basically means no. Haha! Jeff Wu: Don't be so straightforward! <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndIf>> //if小峰路線解失敗 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=-2)>> Jeff Wu: Oh, Jason. Jeff Wu: I'm probably moving out next week. <<#Simple(8)>> ->That's quick. Jeff Wu: If I'm gonna move out sooner or later, I might as well make it sooner. ->Good for you. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: Why? Aren't you living together with Mr. Liao?;<> Jeff Wu: Exactly because of that. Jeff Wu: Our disputes are making it increasingly hard to deal with him, day in, day out. Jeff Wu: I need personal space. Or else I'd start to loathe or hate him.;<> Jeff Wu: I really don't want to do that. Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: But is it necessary to move out...?;<> Jeff Wu: It is. Jeff Wu: I've tried everything possible to negotiate for a middle ground, to make things work. Jeff Wu: He just wouldn't yield. That leaves me no other option. Tyler Cao: I see. Jeff Wu: Moving out is actually good for us both. Jeff Wu: With a bit of distance, we will start to appreciate the bright side of each other. Tyler Cao: Sounds like lovers in a disturbing relationship. Jeff Wu: Relationship with HIM? Ewww! Tyler Cao: Haha. <> <<#EndIf>> //if董欣麗事件最後是小峰認罪 <<#If($SQ_WhoLetHerIn=3)>> Jeff Wu: Hey, since you're both here... Tyler Cao: You what? Jeff Wu: When is the punishment due?;<> Tyler Cao: ...;<> Thought: He's talking about the Sydney Tung case... //if單贏or雙贏 <<#If($FabWinWin=0||$FabWinWin=1)>> Jeff Wu: Wayne is the manager now, but I guess even the manager couldn't save me. <<#EndIf>> Tyler Cao: Okay. I wanted to save this for tomorrow, because you may not be ready for it right now...;<> Jeff Wu: Come on, I already know it's coming. Jeff Wu: I can handle bad news. Try me. Tyler Cao: ...In that case... //if雙贏或單贏 <<#If($FabWinWin=0||$FabWinWin=1)>> Tyler Cao: There's no punishment. Jeff Wu: Huh!? Tyler Cao: Turns out the manager could help you, and he's doing exactly that. Jeff Wu: Then why did you make it sound like a death sentence— Tyler Cao: Because it's fun. Haha. Jeff Wu: You're a dead man! Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: ...It actually feels great, after this emotional roller coaster ride.;<> Tyler Cao: You can thank me later. Tyler Cao: Oh, Wayne said he will assign you extra work to teach you a lesson.;<> Jeff Wu: Sure. Bring it on! Jeff Wu: I've got to thank Wayne in person. <> <<#EndIf>> //if單輸 <<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>> Tyler Cao: The punishment is two months' salary. Jeff Wu: Ugh... Jeff Wu: Okay, I guess I deserve it. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Can they just cut salaries like that? Jeff Wu: Jason, my friend...;<>;<> Jeff Wu: Every time I think you know Vision well enough, you somehow surprise me with naivety. Tyler Cao: According to company rules, no. But you know how they do things.;<>;<> ->Poor you. Jeff Wu: It's not so bad. I thought they were gonna fire me. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: Okay! That's sort of a happen ending! <> <<#EndIf>> <<#EndIf>> Jeff Wu: Hey, enough chitchat. Jeff Wu: Can we help you with something, Jason?;<> <> //if雙贏 <<#If($FabWinWin=0)>> <> <<#EndIf>> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Have you seen Wayne and Justin? ($DLStateFloat);//雙贏路線才跳 Jeff Wu: Speaking of that. Tyler...?;<>;<> Tyler Cao: Oh, I almost forgot. Tyler Cao: Wayne said he and Justin want to see you.;<>;<> Tyler Cao: He said "at the same old place", presuming you know what that means. Thought: Same old place...? Thought: Ah, the roof top. Jeff Wu: Mission accomplished. ->Nothing. <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: OK. See you later, Jason. Tyler Cao: Bye. <<#Simple(8)>> ->Later. // ->It's been an honor working with you. Goodbye. Jeff Wu: ... Tyler Cao: ... Thought: Why are they staring at me like that...? Jeff Wu: *Sigh* Jeff Wu: Are you hiding something from us, Jason? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->What do you mean? ->Nope. <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: You know what? Tyler and I, we've been talking about you since day one. Jeff Wu: You feel somewhat off-ish. Tyler Cao: That's not a nice thing to say.;<>;<> Jeff Wu: Sorry, I don't mean that. Jeff Wu: Then since yesterday, you've been running all over the farm.;<> Tyler Cao: Yeah. Aren't you supposed to formally start next week?;<> Tyler Cao: What are you doing on the farm now? Thought: They are alerted. Jeff Wu: Uh, wait!;<> Jeff Wu: We are basically sure that you're hiding something. Meanwhile, we're totally sure that you're a good man.;<> Jeff Wu: This is not an interrogation. We just want to know that you're OK. Jeff Wu: Because for a second you sounded like you're not coming back.;<>;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I'm all good. Thank you for checking. Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: You're welcome! Tyler Cao: Yes! ->You're right. Maybe I'm not coming back. Jeff Wu: So... Tyler Cao: That was...;<> Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: It's been an honor working with you, Jason.;<> Tyler Cao: Likewise. <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: So be it. Jeff Wu: Farewell. Tyler Cao: Goodbye. <<#Simple(6)>> ->Goodbye. ->... <<#EndSimple>> ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#End>>

title: 結局對話:廖吉興
body:
George Liao: ...
George Liao: You're back.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外)>>
George Liao: ...Listen.
//if篩選失敗
<<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>>
    George Liao: Wayne didn't get the manager's office, but it's alright. Don't blame yourself.
    George Liao: It's not supposed to be an easy win to begin with.
    George Liao: We all gave our best. That's it.
    Thought: ...Is he trying to make me feel better?
    <<#Simple(8)>>
    ->Yeah, I know.
        George Liao: Good.
    ->...Am I supposed to feel better?
        George Liao: I'm just telling you to see things the right way.
        Thought: I guess this is his way of being nice...
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>
//if篩選成功或雙贏
<<#If($FabWinWin=1||$FabWinWin=0)>>
    George Liao: Don't celebrate too hard.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭)>>
    George Liao: There's a tough way up ahead.
    George Liao: Making Wayne the new manager is only the first step.
    George Liao: There are people not happy about it, and old issues we must deal with.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭返回)>>
    Thought: The old man and his endless lectures...
    //if雙贏
    <<#If($FabWinWin=0)>>
        George Liao: But you found a way to make those two mules work together. That's truly something.
        George Liao: Well done.
        Thought: Finally, one "well done" from him.
    <<#EndIf>>
<<#EndIf>>

//if董欣麗事件最後是廖哥頂罪
<<#If($SQ_WhoLetHerIn=-3)>>
    George Liao: You and Tyler did well.
    Thought: ...What?
    George Liao: You didn't tell Jeff about Sydney Tung's case.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外抬手)>>
    George Liao: Now HQ has given the punishment.
    George Liao: Don't mention a word of it. Let this matter quietly go away, will you?;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外抬手返回)>>
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Sure.
        George Liao: Good.
    ->What's the punishment?
        //if雙贏或單贏
        <<#If($FabWinWin=0||$FabWinWin=1)>>
            George Liao: Something surprisingly trivial.
            George Liao: Wayne must be using his manager power. That boy has a good heart.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭)>>
            <<#Simple(10)>>
            ->Wayne is too old to be called a boy.
                George Liao: Not at all.
                George Liao: I still see him as the newbie that he once was.
            ->Does the manager have any power over administrative punishments?
                George Liao: He can't waive it, but making some adjustment is not a problem.
                <<#Simple(8)>>
                ->How do you know he's helping you?
                    George Liao: He helps everyone. That's why he's made the manager.
                ->...
                <<#EndSimple>>
            ->...
            <<#EndSimple>>
            George Liao: OK. Enough about that.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭返回)>>
        <<#EndIf>>
        //if單輸
        <<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>>
            George Liao: I have one more year left before they let me go.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭)>>
            Thought: ...
            George Liao: Not a big deal. I'm about to retire anyway.
            George Liao: Now I'm just going to leave a little bit sooner.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭返回)>>
            Thought: Despite saying that, he's taking it as quite a big deal...
        <<#EndIf>>
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>

//if廖哥小峰和好
<<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>>
    George Liao: ...;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭)>>
    George Liao: Jeff told me.
    George Liao: He's willing to talk to me, more or less because of you.
    George Liao: I'm happy that he's making friends with people like you.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭返回)>>
    George Liao: You are a good influence. Thank you.
    Thought: It's weird to hear the old man say thank you...
<<#EndIf>>


//if篩選組
<<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>>
    George Liao: One last thing.
    George Liao: I owe you a thank you.
    George Liao: I heard about it, how you, Jeff, and Wayne worked together to save my old ass.
    George Liao: Thank you.
    <<#Simple(10)>>
    ->Don't mention it.
    ->It's not just for you.
        George Liao: Manners, young man. You hear a thank you, you say you're welcome.
        Thought: Manners, old man...
    ->Don't forget Tyler.
        George Liao: Tyler? I asked him.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外抬手)>>
        George Liao: He said he's not part of your team.
        Thought: He's lying!;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外抬手返回)>>
    ->...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>

//if廖哥小峰和好
<<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>>
    George Liao: To show my gratitude, I'm inviting you to dinner.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外前傾)>>
    George Liao: Are you coming?
    <<#Simple(99999)>>
    ->Of course.
        George Liao: Good.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外前傾返回)>>
        George Liao: I will pick a restaurant and let you know.
    ->Probably not. I'm kinda busy.
    ->Depends on the restaurant.
        George Liao: It's classy, in case you're worrying about that.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外前傾返回)>>
        George Liao: I'm a man of class.
        George Liao: I will pick a restaurant and let you know.
        Thought: Hmm...
    <<#EndSimple>>
<<#EndIf>>


//if篩選
<<#If($MCBelongSelectDepartment=1)>>
    George Liao: You're about to be a part of our team.
<<#EndIf>>
//if操作
<<#If($MCBelongControlDepartment=1)>>
    George Liao: You're about to be a part of our farm.
<<#EndIf>>
George Liao: I suppose you've seen enough to know it's not an easy job.
George Liao: Be prepared.
Thought: You don't know yet, but today is actually my last day...
George Liao: ...;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭)>>
George Liao: Life is tough for you youngsters...
George Liao: Fewer jobs, worse pay. It's not like the good old days anymore.
George Liao: But I can tell you're trying, striving. That's a good thing.
George Liao: ...
George Liao: Work hard and you will have a future.;<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外轉頭返回)>>
George Liao: There. I've said my piece.
George Liao: You're free to go now.
<<#Simple(8)>>
->Bye.
    //
->It's been an honor working with you.
    George Liao: ...
    George Liao: Good manners. Good.
    George Liao: Make it a habit.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
<<Play(廖吉興,MBU_廖吉興_側坐看外返回)>>
<<#End>>

body: //這段寫的ˊ還行，看雙贏要不要也能晉這段。 //把胖胖改成坐在旁邊，打遊戲話題有callout一下球球 <> //if操作失敗 <<#If($FabWinWin=-1)>> Paxton Luo: Ah, Jayson...;<> Thought: He's very much defeated... Paxton Luo: What's gonna happen? Do you think some will quit? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Probably. Paxton Luo: Figured.;<> Paxton Luo: I'd stay and fight, but I guess the same can't be said for everyone else. Why!? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Because of reasons. ->Sorry, I can't tell you. <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: ... Paxton Luo: ...I see.;<> Paxton Luo: It's a shame... ->I have a second chance if I work hard enough. Paxton Luo: Really!? Paxton Luo: That's great! Paxton Luo: I'd feel very sorry if they let you go.;<> <<#EndSimple>> Paxton Luo: ... Paxton Luo: I'm just feeling bad about this situation... Paxton Luo: For the very few friends that I have, I can't help clinging tightly to them.;<> Thought: He's talking about Justin, I guess. title: 自己去跟小峰對質
body: Jeff Wu: Hey, Jason! What's up? Thought: Should I tell him about Mr. Liao? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Nothing. Jeff Wu: Huh? Jeff Wu: I'm in the middle of something. Go find QQ if you just want to hang out. <<#End>> ->Mr. Liao turned himself in, saying he helped Sydney Tung pass our prescreening measures. Jeff Wu: George? But—!? Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: How did you know about that? <<#Simple(99999)>> ->Tyler and I have been investigating the case all along. ->He came to Tyler and I happened to be around. <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: ...;<> Jeff Wu: It's requested by HQ, isn't it? Jeff Wu: They must catch the mole and seal loopholes. Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: Why are you telling me this?;<> <<#Simple(99999)>> ->I just think you'd want to know. // ->Because I believe he is taking the fall for you. Jeff Wu: ... <<#Simple(3)>> ->Am I right? ->... <<#EndSimple>> <<#EndSimple>> Jeff Wu: I— Jeff Wu: FUCK!!;<> <<#EndSimple>> <> <<#Wait(3)>> Jeff Wu: Tyler! <> //這段對話跟另一組很接近，但是這時候兩人是站著的 Tyler Cao: W-what's up? Jeff Wu: I know everything! Tyler Cao: Wait, Jeff. What are you talking about!? Jeff Wu: I can't let George take the fall for me!;<> Tyler Cao: Calm down, Jeff. Jeff Wu: ... Tyler Cao: Jason told you everything?;<> Thought: Tyler's looking at me, accusingly... Jeff Wu: ...Yeah.;<>;<> Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Okay, listen, Jeff...;<> Tyler Cao: We can keep Mr. Liao out of this, if you step up now. Jeff Wu: Good... Tyler Cao: But to invalidate his confession, I will need your side of the story, the whole truth. Jeff Wu: ...;<> Jeff Wu: Ms. Tung helped us a lot.;<> Jeff Wu: A few days ago she came to George, saying she needed money to visit her grandson. Jeff Wu: As we all know, George doesn't have that much money to spare. So she asked for a contributor job... Jeff Wu: But George said no. Jeff Wu: She was heartbroken. So I thought...;<> Jeff Wu: ... Tyler Cao: Why was Mr. Liao hanging around when Jason was interviewing Tung? Just coincidence? I'm not buying it.;<> Jeff Wu: ...He wanted to stop me. Jeff Wu: Assigning her to Jason was his doing, too.;<> Jeff Wu: The idea was that if she's not admitted, no one would find out my little trick. Jeff Wu: Looks like it's not that simple, though...;<> Tyler Cao: ... Jeff Wu: What's the punishment for me? Tyler Cao: ...I don't know yet.;<> Tyler Cao: But chances are you won't be able to keep this job. Jeff Wu: Huh...;<>;<> Jeff Wu: But George will be left alone, yes? Tyler Cao: Yeah. Jeff Wu: Good.;<> Jeff Wu: I will talk to him, then. Jeff Wu: It's not right to ruin his life because of my shenanigans. Jeff Wu: You just write the report based on the truth, Tyler. Tyler Cao: ...OK. Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: George Liao, old grumpy guy with a poopy face.;<> Jeff Wu: You know what? He was not like that before. Once he was a very helpful not-so-old guy. Jeff Wu: Annoyingly so. He used to actively offer help to anyone who might need it, like some sort of salesman of good intentions.;<> Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: Then I don't know what happened. Suddenly he became this selfish man.;<> Tyler Cao: Jeff...;<> Jeff Wu: No, I happened. He became what he is because of me. Tyler Cao: ... Jeff Wu: My mom passed away, leaving me to George. He raised me like his own son. Jeff Wu: He actually could afford to lend Ms. Tung the money she needed. Jeff Wu: But all his savings were set a side in an emergency fund for me.;<> Jeff Wu: I did argue with him, tell him that I don't need the money. Unsurprisingly, he wouldn't listen. //if廖哥小峰好結局 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=2)>> Jeff Wu: We've been having fights, yeah. Jeff Wu: You just saw one yourself, Jason. Haha...;<> Jeff Wu: Anyway, I've said my piece. Jeff Wu: I think we know each other a lot better than before, George and me. Jeff Wu: I broke the rules to let Ms. Tung through. Of course I'd take the consequences.;<> <<#GoTo(@跳過完全沒解廖哥小峰的可能2)>> <<#EndIf>> //if廖哥小峰壞結局 <<#If($FonLiauEventStatus=-2)>> Jeff Wu: We've been having fights, yeah. Jeff Wu: You just saw one yourself, Jason. Haha...;<> Jeff Wu: Anyway, I've said my piece. Jeff Wu: Between George and I, there should be a healthy distance, like, what's his and what's mine.;<> Jeff Wu: In this case, the consequences are all mine. He has no right to take them for me. Jeff Wu: He wants to protect me, alright, but that's for the peace of his own conscience. Jeff Wu: I won't allow him to manipulate my life anymore, for good or bad. Jeff Wu: I take my own consequences. This is a good start. <<#GoTo(@跳過完全沒解廖哥小峰的可能2)>> <<#EndIf>> //if沒解廖哥小峰支線 Jeff Wu: ... Jeff Wu: Sorry for boring you out.;<> Jeff Wu: Now that you've had my confession, I guess it's case closed? Tyler Cao: Yeah. Jeff Wu: I made the decision, I take the consequence, fair and square.;<> <<@跳過完全沒解廖哥小峰的可能2>> Jeff Wu: Let's see what HQ wants to do with me. Tyler Cao: I—;<> Jeff Wu: Don't you blame yourself, Tyler Cao.;<> Jeff Wu: I screwed myself up royally. Simple as that. Tyler Cao: ...;<> Jeff Wu: Ha! I knew you're blaming yourself. Jeff Wu: OK, then. Jeff Wu: Now if you folks excuse me, I have work to do.;<> Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Sure. Tyler Cao: Thank you, Jeff. <> //回小峰位子 <<#Wait(3)>> Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: I'm glad that you had a talk with Jeff.;<> <<#Simple(6)>> ->Why's that? // ->...I thought you'd hate me for it. Tyler Cao: I did, a little bit, in the beginning, but... ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I'd regret it for a lifetime if we didn't hear him out. Tyler Cao: And what he said at the end of it...;<> Tyler Cao: That took a massive load off me. Tyler Cao: ... Tyler Cao: Now I'm sure we have the best of all possible outcomes.;<> Tyler Cao: Thank you, Jason. Tyler Cao: I owe you one. Tyler Cao: Let me know if I can ever return this favor. <<#Simple(8)>> ->No need. Tyler Cao: Haha, maybe for now. Just remember that the offer stands. ->I'll make a note of it. Tyler Cao: Yeah. ->... <<#EndSimple>> Tyler Cao: I'll go back to work then. The report won't write itself. Tyler Cao: I guess you have work to do, as well. Tyler Cao: See you later. <> <> <<#End>>

title: 倒地實驗員對談
body:
Chelsea Ke: Wait...
Chelsea Ke: Stop...
Thought: Is she lab staff?
<<#Simple(12)>>
->Who are you?
->Stay down and shut up.
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Chelsea Ke: ...
Chelsea Ke: You want to destroy this lab?
<<#Simple(12)>>
->Correct.
->You have a problem?
->...
<<#EndSimple>>
Chelsea Ke: Please, don't...
Chelsea Ke: You just saw our work for the first time.
Chelsea Ke: Shock and repulse... It's all normal.
Chelsea Ke: I was like that when I first came here.
Chelsea Ke: Give it more...time...
Chelsea Ke: You will...get used to it.
Thought: She's passing out.
<<#Simple(99999)>>
->I can give it centuries and it's still wrong.
->No sane person could get used to that.
    Chelsea Ke: ...
    Chelsea Ke: No, I'm—
->Personally, I don't think it's that bad.
    Chelsea Ke: ...
    Chelsea Ke: ...Why do you pretend you care, then?
    Chelsea Ke: ...Because of peer pressure?
    Chelsea Ke: Stupid... You're...one of us...
<<#EndSimple>>
Chelsea Ke: ...
Chelsea Ke: Behind this door is the hard work of many, many brilliant minds.
Chelsea Ke: ...;//躺下去
Chelsea Ke: So be it. Do what you want.
Chelsea Ke: You cannot stop us.
Chelsea Ke: History is on our side.
Chelsea Ke: You...will...know...
//實驗員昏過去
Thought: She passed out.
<<#End>>
